creator: karpes@rpi.edu
text:
The latest build appears to work fine.

Thanks!

--Simon

On Wed, 22 Sep 1999, Chris Piazza wrote:

> On Wed, Sep 22, 1999 at 03:48:05PM -0400, Simon Karpen wrote:
> > Chris -
> > 
> > Using the fetchmail port of a couple days ago, I managed to get
> > fetchmail to build by adding -lcom_err to the list of libraries. 
> > It seems that the functions it's complaining about are in libcom_err
> > and it's not automatically picking up libcom_err when kerberos is
> > linked in. 
> > 
> > This appears to affect other ports, including xlockmore and probably a 
> > number of others. I'm going to see if there's some sort of general
> > fix in the ports rules, but I doubt it.
> 
> Thanks!  I updated the port to fetchmail 5.1.0 and I added -lcom_err.
> Please tell me if this fixes the problem; I might have put it
> in the wrong place.
> 
> -Chris
> --
